Identification.LongTitle: An Act to amalgamate The Roman Catholic Episcopal Corporation of Ottawa and The Roman Catholic Episcopal Corporation for the Diocese of Alexandria-Cornwall, in Ontario, Canada
Identification.ShortTitle: The Roman Catholic Episcopal Corporation for the Diocese of Ottawa-Cornwall Act
Identification.RunningHead: The Roman Catholic Episcopal Corporation for the Diocese of Ottawa-Cornwall Act

Provision.Text: This enactment provides for the incorporation of The Roman Catholic Episcopal Corporation for the Diocese of Ottawa-Cornwall, an entity to be formed by the amalgamation of The Roman Catholic Episcopal Corporation of Ottawa and The Roman Catholic Episcopal Corporation for the Diocese of Alexandria-Cornwall, in Ontario, Canada.
Whereas The Roman Catholic Episcopal Corporation of Ottawa was incorporated by an Act of the Parliament of Canada, being chapter 104 of the Statutes of Canada, 1884;

Whereas The Roman Catholic Episcopal Corporation for the Diocese of Alexandria-Cornwall, in Ontario, Canada, was incorporated by an Act of the Legislative Assembly of the Province of Ontario, being chapter 98 of the Statutes of the Province of Ontario, 1891, which itself was amended by a further Act of that Legislature, being chapter 100 of the Statutes of the Province of Ontario, 1977;
Whereas His Holiness Pope Francis has seen fit to amalgamate the Archdiocese of Ottawa and the Diocese of Alexandria-Cornwall at canon law by papal bull dated May 6, 2020;
And whereas the above-mentioned corporations have by their petition prayed for an Act of incorporation amalgamating the said corporations into a single entity at Canadian law;
Now, therefore, His Majesty, by and with the advice and consent of the Senate and House of Commons of Canada, enacts as follows:

Section.Text: This Act may be cited as The Roman Catholic Episcopal Corporation of Ottawa-Cornwall Act.
The following definitions apply in this Act.

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: The Roman Catholic Episcopal Corporation of Ottawa incorporated under Chapter 104 of the Statutes of Canada, 1884; and

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: The Roman Catholic Episcopal Corporation for the Diocese of Alexandria-Cornwall, in Ontario, Canada, which was incorporated by an Act of the Legislative Assembly of the Province of Ontario, being chapter 98 of the Statutes of the Province of Ontario, 1891, which itself was amended by a further Act of that Legislature, being chapter 100 of the Statutes of the Province of Ontario, 1977. (corporations fusionnantes)

Definition.Text: Corporation means The Roman Catholic Episcopal Corporation of Ottawa-Cornwall as amalgamated under subsection 3(1) of this Act. (Corporation)

Subsection.Text: The Roman Catholic Episcopal Corporation for the Diocese of Alexandria-Cornwall, in Ontario, Canada, for which a certificate of continuance under subsection 211(1) of the Canada Not-for-profit Corporations Act is deemed to have been issued under subsection 10(3) of this Act, is amalgamated with The Roman Catholic Episcopal Corporation of Ottawa to form a body corporate to be known as âThe Roman Catholic Episcopal Corporation of Ottawa-Cornwallâ in English and âLa Corporation Ã©piscopale catholique romaine dâOttawa-Cornwallâ in French.
For greater certainty, the Corporation is a corporation without share capital incorporated by a special Act of Parliament and not continued under any other Act.
The Roman Catholic Archbishop of the Archdiocese of Ottawa-Cornwall, in communion with the Roman Catholic Church, is the sole member of the Corporation.
The Corporationâs head office is to be at Ottawa, in the Province of Ontario, or in such other city as the Archbishop or his successors take up personal residence.

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: the property of each of the amalgamating corporations becomes the property of the Corporation;

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: the Corporation becomes liable for the obligations of each of the amalgamating corporations;

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: a cause of action or claim against or liability of either of the amalgamating corporations that exists immediately prior to the coming into force of this Act becomes a cause of action or claim against or liability of the Corporation;

Paragraph.Label: (d)
Paragraph.Text: a civil, criminal or administrative action or proceeding that has been taken or could have been taken by or against either of the amalgamating corporations immediately prior to the coming into force of this Act may be continued or taken by or against the Corporation until the time limit that would have applied if the amalgamation had not taken place;

Paragraph.Label: (e)
Paragraph.Text: a conviction against or a ruling, order or judgment in favour of or against either of the amalgamating corporations may be enforced by or against the Corporation;

Paragraph.Label: (f)
Paragraph.Text: the claims, rights and privileges of the amalgamating corporations become the claims, rights and privileges of the Corporation; and

Paragraph.Label: (g)
Paragraph.Text: any devise, testament, bequest, donation, beneficial trust or other instrument transmitting property for the benefit of the amalgamating corporations is deemed to transmit such benefits to the Corporation.

Section.Text: The objects of the Corporation are to

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: care for the faithful of the diocese;

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: provide for the spiritual needs of those in the diocese;

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: foster vocations to different ministries and to consecrated life;

Paragraph.Label: (d)
Paragraph.Text: govern diocesan affairs in accordance with the tenets of the Roman Catholic faith; and

Paragraph.Label: (e)
Paragraph.Text: administer the property, business and other temporal affairs of the Corporation.

Section.Text: The Corporation has all the rights, powers and privileges of a natural person.

Subsection.Text: The Corporation is a not-for-profit corporation carrying out its objects without pecuniary gain to a member of the Corporation.
No income or other property, real or personal, of the Corporation is payable to or otherwise available for the personal benefit of a member of the Corporation.
The Archbishop may call upon or appoint any advisers or assistants that are necessary for carrying out the Corporationâs objects.

The Archbishop may not make or execute any deed, mortgage, conveyance, demise, release or assignment of the whole or any part of the Corporationâs real property without the written consent of one member of the diocesan clergy and one lay person from the Archdiocese of Ottawa-Cornwall appointed by the Archbishop for this purpose.
Any deed, mortgage, conveyance, demise, release or assignment related to real property or any interest in real property is deemed to be executed if

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: the Corporationâs seal or the Archbishopâs seal or signature appears on the deed, mortgage, conveyance, demise, release or assignment; and

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: a declaration that the instrument has been executed according to subsection (2) is affixed to the deed, mortgage, conveyance, demise, release or assignment.

Section.Text: If the Archbishop is incapacitated, the person or persons administering the diocese during the Archbishopâs incapacity may exercise the powers conferred by this Act on the Archbishop.

Subsection.Text: Words and expressions used in this section have the same meaning as in the Canada Not-for-profit Corporations Act.

The Roman Catholic Episcopal Corporation for the Diocese of Alexandria-Cornwall, in Ontario, Canada, is deemed to have applied to the Director for a certificate of continuance under subsection 211(1) of the Canada Not-for-profit Corporations Act on the day before the day on which this Act receives royal assent.

Notwithstanding any provision of the Canada Not-for-profit Corporations Act, the Director is deemed to have issued a certificate of continuance to The Roman Catholic Episcopal Corporation for the Diocese of Alexandria-Cornwall, in Ontario, Canada, in accordance with section 276 of the Canada Not-for-profit Corporations Act on the day on which this Act receives royal assent.

Section.Text: For the purposes of subsection 211(7) of the Canada Not-for-profit Corporations Act, this Act serves as the certificate of continuance.
An Act relating to the Roman Catholic Diocese of Ottawa, chapter 104 of the Statutes of Canada, 1884, is repealed.
